prevalence.source: Indian rheumatology epidemiology syntheses + metabolic risk context
prevalence.trend: increasing with urbanization, obesity, and CKD

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical flares; crystal confirmation when available; serum urate supportive but not diagnostic during acute attack

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate due to follow-up gaps and variable ULT titration

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Mostly clinical diagnosis; limited access to arthrocentesis in many settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: NSAIDs/colchicine/steroids for flares; allopurinol or febuxostat for urate lowering; lifestyle counseling
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Under-treatment and inconsistent treat-to-target titration; comorbidity burden and follow-up fragmentation
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic ULT and colchicine availability
